## Executive Summary
- **Field Pulse**: Five World-Action Model (WAM) papers dropped in the past week — and not one of them measures downstream policy success rate. The evaluation gap is now the field's single most urgent open problem.
- **Must-Read**: DeepMind's *From AGI to ASI* (arXiv:2606.12683) — a 60-page roadmap covering multi-agent collectives as an ASI pathway, directly motivating distributed world model research.
- **Your Focus Today**: Commit `Planning/WorldBench_Design.md`. This is **day 4** it has been priority #1. The stub is 15 minutes of writing. Do it before reading anything else.

## Top 3 Papers to Read

*(No arXiv scan output available today — citing from blog and ideas cross-references)*

1. **AHEAD: Action-Conditioned World Models via Latent Feature Prediction** — 4.9M-param augmentation that outperforms 7B pixel-level baselines on real robot tasks; the architecture WMATS should start with and the clearest empirical signal that FVD diverges from task success.
   [arXiv:2606.02486] · Relevance: ★★★★★

2. **WorldOlympiad: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Physical World Models** — Three evaluation tracks (physical, geometric, interactive) — all in video output space, not policy outcome space. Study it to understand the gap your benchmark closes.
   [arXiv:2606.11129] · Relevance: ★★★★☆

3. **From AGI to ASI** (Google DeepMind, Genewein, Legg, Hutter et al.) — 4 pathways including multi-agent collectives and recursive self-improvement; motivates studying how to compose world models across agents.
   [arXiv:2606.12683] · Relevance: ★★★☆☆

## Lab Highlights (What the Big Players Did)

- **Google DeepMind**: Released a 60-page *From AGI to ASI* roadmap with four transition pathways — notably, the multi-agent collective pathway (networks of AGI agents whose combined intelligence exceeds any individual) directly motivates research on distributed/shared world models and collective embodied intelligence. → *Potential long-term framing for WorldBench: multi-agent policy evaluation as a future WMATS extension.*

- **NVIDIA**: GraspGen-X (ICRA 2026, arXiv:2606.00998) achieves zero-shot generalization to novel gripper morphologies never seen in training by representing each gripper as its **Swept Volume** — the 3D region traced during a grasp. A clean geometry-aware encoding that makes cross-embodiment transfer work without domain adaptation. → *Directly inspires the Geometry-Augmented World Model idea: encode swept volumes + contact surfaces as world model conditioning channels — physically grounded by construction, not by post-hoc correction.*

- **NVIDIA**: ScheduleStream (ICRA 2026) — GPU-accelerated multi-arm task and motion planning using learned samplers. → *Strong evidence that multi-arm coordination is tractable; a world model serving as the dynamics oracle in a sampler-based planner is a natural next step.*

- **Anthropic**: Released Public Record survey results (~52K Americans). #1 fear: job displacement (64%). #2: cognitive dependency (56%). Only 15% trust AI companies to self-regulate. → *Low direct relevance for the research, but useful for framing the assistive embodied AI value proposition when writing grant applications or papers.*

## GitHub Watch

- **[LogosRoboticsGroup/Metis](https://github.com/LogosRoboticsGroup/Metis)** — Created **yesterday** (June 14). Proposes a "World-Action Model for end-to-end autonomous driving and urban navigation." Details still sparse — check if an arXiv link appears today. If the architecture uses temporal asymmetry (like AHA-WAM), this is independent validation that temporal horizon separation is domain-agnostic, which strengthens any paper making that claim.

- **[bchao1/foveated_diffusion](https://github.com/bchao1/foveated_diffusion)** — Spatially adaptive compute for diffusion (high-res in attended regions, low-res in periphery). Appeared in *two consecutive* GitHub scans — signal growing. Immediately applicable as a AHEAD patch-attention mask concentrating compute on end-effector + object ROI. If foveation raises WMATS while lowering FVD, that is a direct second data point for the FVD-utility decorrelation claim.

- **[StaryMoon/Pi0-Unofficial](https://github.com/StaryMoon/Pi0-Unofficial)** — Clean PyTorch reproduction of π₀ (Physical Intelligence's flow-matching VLA). Useful as a reference implementation for flow-matching ablations without needing proprietary code access.

- **[LTX-2-3-desktop/LTX-2.3](https://github.com/LTX-2-3-desktop/LTX-2.3)** — Open, Veo-3-quality diffusion-transformer video model with GGUF local inference. Lowest-friction way to run high-quality video generation locally — useful for Cosmos failure taxonomy experiments if you want a quality comparison baseline.

## Today's Best Research Idea

**WMATS-Bench: The First Policy-Utility Benchmark for World-Action Models**

Five WAM architectures (AHEAD, Efficient-WAM, AHA-WAM, Flash-WAM, Cosmos 3) appeared in a single week — all benchmarked on video quality, zero on downstream task success rate. WMATS = policy success rate *with* WM rollouts / success rate *without* WM rollouts, measured on held-out manipulation tasks with a fixed Diffusion Policy backbone. A paper that runs all five through this protocol becomes the canonical ranking that every future WAM paper must cite. The competitive value grows quadratically with each new WAM that enters the field without a policy evaluation.

Quick experiment: Set up OpenEnv + MuJoCo peg insertion + Diffusion Policy. Run the **no-WM baseline** (condition A): zero world model rollouts, train for 10K real transitions, record success rate at 5K and 10K steps, 3 seeds. One number — e.g., "9/30 at 10K steps" — is the anchor that turns this from a hypothetical into an experiment. GPU time: ~4 H100 hours.

## Research Radar

★ **Heating up**: World-Action Model (WAM) architectures — 5 papers in 1 week, field converging fast on feature-space prediction over pixel-space; temporal asymmetry as a recurring design principle.

☆ **Opportunity**: Policy-utility evaluation — WMATS gap is now *wider* with every new WAM that lacks downstream evaluation. The foveated world model direction (spatial ROI masking) is unexplored by any WAM paper. Tactile/proprioceptive conditioning is unaddressed despite NVIDIA's 500K-trajectory Physical AI Dataset having force/torque logs.

⚠ **Crowded / Deprioritize**: VLA architecture from scratch (saturated — Uni-LaViRA, GR00T N1.6, unofficial π₀ all dropped this week); WAM inference efficiency (Flash-WAM, Efficient-WAM, AHA-WAM already attacked this from multiple angles); photorealistic video generation (LTX-2.3 is Veo-3 quality and open — you cannot compete on raw generation quality); multi-agent world models this quarter (DeepMind's $10M safety funding call deadline Aug 8 will flood this space by Q1 2027 — wait for the first wave to see the gaps).
